creator: a.kupries@westend.com
text:
> >Submitter-Id:   current-users
 > >Originator:     Mikhail Teterin
 > >Organization:   Virtual Estates, Inc.
 > >Confidential:   no
 > >Synopsis:       Tcl Data transformations (Tcl-Trf) port
 > >Severity:       non-critical
 > >Priority:       medium
 > >Category:       ports
 > >Release:        FreeBSD 3.3-STABLE i386
 > >Class:          change-request
 > >Environment: 
 > 
 > 	Can be built against either Tcl-8.0 or 8.2, but
 > 		NEEDS PLUS PATCHES
 > 	The sooner the standard TCL/TK ports incorporate the plus-patches
 > 	from http://www.worldaccess.nl/~nijtmans/plus.html the better for
 > 	the world peace.
 
 Clarification. Can be built against 8.0., 8.1. or 8.2.
 Differences:
 
 8.0.:	Either needs the Plus-Patches or the patch coming with the
 	source distribution to compile and work.
 
 
 8.1.:	Compiles and works without patches, but the functionality
 	based upon the patches is switched off (at runtime! Using the
 	lib with a patched 8.1 automatically reactivates this
 	functionality).
 
 	Uses #ifdef/#define tricks to allow compilation against
 	unpatched 8.1. 
 
 	Queries the stubs table to allow (de)activation of
 	functionality requiring patchess to the core.
 
 
 8.2.:	No patches required, the functionality contained in them moved
 	into the core.
 
 -- 
 Sincerely,
 	Andreas Kupries <a.kupries@westend.com>
 			<http://www.purl.org/NET/akupries/>
 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
